name: Jam Miller
headline: Late-round Alabama back buried behind Henderson and Stevenson
body:
A seventh-round pick out of Alabama, Miller (245th overall) lands in a crowded New England backfield that already runs through TreVeyon Henderson and Rhamondre Stevenson. That leaves him fighting for RB3 work with no clear path to touches unless an injury opens the rotation. The projections reflect it: roughly 34 carries for 156 yards and a score, plus a couple of catches, all adding up to about 24 fantasy points across the year.

The profile is more concerning than the depth chart alone. His efficiency dipped to 3.9 yards per carry with a visible loss of burst, and the supporting skills lag, including shaky pass protection (seven pressures allowed on 49 snaps) and five drops over two years. He fits the gap-scheme run game on paper, but with Stevenson signed for the foreseeable future, Miller is a stash at best and essentially undraftable in standard leagues right now.
